message:
feat(cli): add ntm grep command for pane output search

Implements regex-based search across tmux pane output buffers with
ripgrep-style interface and features:

Search capabilities:
- Full regex pattern support via Go's regexp package
- Case-insensitive search (-i / --ignore-case)
- Inverted matching (-v / --invert-match)
- Configurable search depth (-n / --max-lines, default 1000)

Context display:
- Lines before match (-B / --before-context)
- Lines after match (-A / --after-context)
- Combined context (-C / --context)
- Grep-like output format with line numbers

Filtering options:
- Agent type filters: --cc, --cod, --gmi
- All sessions mode: --all
- Files-with-matches mode (-l) for pane listing only
- Session-specific search when name provided

Output:
- Human-readable colorized output with match highlighting
- JSON output (--json) for scripting/automation
- Match count and pane statistics

Implementation details:
- GrepMatch/GrepResult types with Text()/JSON() output methods
- AgentFilter struct for consistent pane filtering
- Integration with existing tmux.CapturePaneOutput
- Uses theme system for consistent coloring

Usage:
  ntm grep 'error' myproject          # All panes
  ntm grep 'def.*auth' myproject -i   # Case insensitive
  ntm grep 'TODO' myproject -C 3      # With context
  ntm grep 'pattern' --all            # All sessions

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-bke.1","title":"Project Config Discovery (.ntm/)","description":"Auto-detect and load .ntm/ directory configuration from project roots.

## Overview
When running NTM commands in a project directory, automatically discover and load .ntm/config.toml to customize behavior per-project.

## Directory Structure
```
myproject/
├── .ntm/
│   ├── config.toml      # Project settings
│   ├── palette.md       # Project commands
│   └── templates/       # Project templates
├── src/
└── ...
```

## Discovery Algorithm
```go
func FindProjectConfig(startDir string) (*ProjectConfig, error) {
    dir := startDir
    for {
        configPath := filepath.Join(dir, ".ntm", "config.toml")
        if fileExists(configPath) {
            return LoadProjectConfig(configPath)
        }
        
        parent := filepath.Dir(dir)
        if parent == dir { // Reached root
            return nil, nil // No project config
        }
        dir = parent
    }
}
```

## Config Merge Order
1. Hardcoded defaults (in binary)
2. Global config (~/.config/ntm/config.toml)
3. Project config (.ntm/config.toml)
4. CLI flags

Later sources override earlier ones.

```go
func LoadMergedConfig() *Config {
    cfg := DefaultConfig()
    
    if global, err := LoadGlobalConfig(); err == nil {
        cfg = MergeConfig(cfg, global)
    }
    
    if project, err := FindProjectConfig(cwd); err == nil && project != nil {
        cfg = MergeConfig(cfg, project)
    }
    
    return cfg
}
```

## Project Config Schema
```toml
# .ntm/config.toml

# Default agent counts when spawning
[defaults]
agents = { cc = 2, cod = 1 }  # No Gemini for this project

# Project palette extends global
[palette]
file = "palette.md"  # Relative to .ntm/

# Project templates extend global
[templates]
dir = "templates"  # Relative to .ntm/

# Custom agent commands for this project
[agents]
# Override default Claude command
claude = "claude --project /path/to/claude-config"
```

## Init Command
```bash
ntm config project init           # Create .ntm/ scaffold
ntm config project init --force   # Overwrite existing
```

Creates:
```
.ntm/
├── config.toml    # With comments explaining options
├── palette.md     # Empty palette scaffold
└── templates/     # Empty directory
```

## Detection in Commands
All commands that use config should call merged config:
```go
func newSpawnCmd() *cobra.Command {
    return &cobra.Command{
        RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
            cfg := config.LoadMerged() // <-- Uses project config if present
            // ...
        },
    }
}
```

## Files to Modify/Create
- internal/config/project.go - Project config discovery
- internal/config/merge.go - Config merging logic
- internal/cli/config.go - Add 'project init' subcommand","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:23.935269-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:23.935269-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-bke.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-bke","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:23.936439-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-c2b","title":"EPIC: Plugin & Extension System","description":"Allow users to extend NTM with custom agents, commands, and hooks.

## Problem Statement

NTM currently supports three hardcoded agent types: Claude, Codex, Gemini.
Users may want to use other agents or customize behavior:

1. **Other AI Agents**: Cursor, Aider, Continue, OpenAI API, local LLMs
2. **Custom Workflows**: Project-specific commands and automation
3. **Integration Hooks**: Run scripts before/after NTM operations
4. **Team Customization**: Share agent configs across a team

Currently, users must fork NTM to add new agent types or modify behavior.

## Goals

1. Allow custom agent definitions via config files
2. Support custom commands as executable scripts
3. Provide hook system for pre/post command execution
4. Make plugins discoverable and easy to install
5. Document plugin development clearly

## Why This Matters

Extensibility enables:
- Immediate support for any new AI coding agent
- Project-specific workflows without code changes
- Team-shared configurations and standards
- Community plugin ecosystem
- Future-proofing against agent landscape changes

## Success Criteria

- Users can define custom agents in ~/.config/ntm/agents/
- ntm spawn myproject --cursor=2 (custom agent)
- Users can add commands via ~/.config/ntm/commands/
- ntm my-custom-command (runs script)
- Hooks fire at defined extension points
- ntm plugins list shows installed plugins

## Plugin Types

### 1. Agent Plugins (~/.config/ntm/agents/*.toml)

Define new agent types:

# cursor.toml
[agent]
name = "cursor"
alias = "cur"
command = "cursor --headless --ai-mode"
description = "Cursor AI editor"
default_tags = ["cursor", "editor"]

Usage: ntm spawn myproject --cursor=2

### 2. Command Plugins (~/.config/ntm/commands/*)

Executable scripts become commands:

#\!/bin/bash
# ~/.config/ntm/commands/sync-all
# Description: Sync all agents with latest code

for session in $(ntm list --json | jq -r '.[].name'); do
  ntm send "$session" --all "git pull && review changes"
done

Usage: ntm sync-all

### 3. Hook Plugins (~/.config/ntm/hooks/*)

Run before/after NTM commands:

# hooks.toml
[hooks.pre_send]
command = "~/.config/ntm/hooks/log-prompt.sh"
pass_args = true

[hooks.post_spawn]
command = "notify-send 'NTM' 'Session created'"

## Technical Approach

Plugin Discovery:
- Scan known directories on startup
- Cache plugin metadata for performance
- Reload on ntm plugins refresh

Agent Plugin Loading:
- Parse TOML to AgentConfig struct
- Register with spawn/add commands
- Generate shell integration aliases

Command Plugin Execution:
- Discover executables in commands/
- Run with NTM context in environment
- Pass JSON via stdin, capture stdout/stderr

Hook Execution:
- Define extension points in each command
- Run hooks synchronously (pre) or async (post)
- Pass command context as JSON
- Fail-safe: hook errors don't break NTM

## Security Considerations

- Plugins are user-installed, user-trusted
- No automatic plugin download/installation
- Clear documentation of execution context
- Hooks can see all command arguments
- Consider --no-hooks flag for sensitive operations

## Interaction with Other Features

- JSON Output: Plugin commands can use --json
- Config: Plugins extend config schema
- Remote: Plugins work locally, commands sent to remote
- Tags: Custom agents can have default tags

## Future Possibilities

- Plugin registry/marketplace
- Version pinning for plugins
- Plugin dependencies
- Programmatic plugin API (Go plugins)

-{"id":"ntm-c36","title":"Implement --robot-health for agent health matrix","description":"Focused health check returning responsiveness and activity metrics.

## Rationale
--robot-snapshot includes health data, but sometimes I just need a quick health check without the full state. This is the lightweight "are things working" query.

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "checked_at": "ISO8601",
  "system": {
    "tmux_ok": true,
    "disk_free_gb": 42.5,
    "load_avg": 1.2
  },
  "sessions": {
    "myproject": {
      "healthy": true,
      "agents": {
        "0.1": {"responsive": true, "output_rate": "high", "last_activity_sec": 2},
        "0.2": {"responsive": true, "output_rate": "low", "last_activity_sec": 45},
        "0.3": {"responsive": false, "issue": "no_output_5min"}
      }
    }
  },
  "alerts": ["0.3: no output 5min"]
}
```

## Health Heuristics
- responsive: has pane produced output recently AND is tmux pane alive
- output_rate: high (>1 line/sec), medium, low (<1 line/min), none
- last_activity_sec: time since last output line

## Alert Conditions
- No output for 5 minutes (configurable)
- Pane process exited
- High CPU in pane (if detectable)
- Disk space low
- Session unexpectedly killed

## Use Cases
1. Quick sanity check before sending batch instructions
2. Detect stuck agents for restart
3. System health monitoring
4. Pre-flight check before spawning more agents","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlackSnow","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:51:22.401882-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T09:21:33.14533-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-ceb","title":"Create internal/agentmail HTTP client package","description":"# Create internal/agentmail HTTP Client Package

## Purpose

Foundation for all Agent Mail integration. This package provides a clean Go interface to the MCP Agent Mail HTTP API.

## Technical Details

### Package Structure
```
internal/agentmail/
├── client.go       # HTTP client with connection pooling, retries
├── types.go        # Go structs matching Agent Mail data model
├── tools.go        # Methods for each MCP tool (send_message, fetch_inbox, etc.)
├── resources.go    # Methods for MCP resources (resource://inbox, etc.)
├── errors.go       # Error types with Agent Mail error codes
└── client_test.go  # Unit tests with mock server
```

### Client Configuration
```go
type Client struct {
    baseURL     string       // Default: http://127.0.0.1:8765/mcp/
    bearerToken string       // From AGENT_MAIL_TOKEN or config
    httpClient  *http.Client // With timeouts, connection pooling
    projectKey  string       // Cached project path
}

func NewClient(opts ...Option) (*Client, error)
func (c *Client) IsAvailable() bool  // Health check
```

### Key Types (matching Agent Mail schema)
```go
type Agent struct {
    ID              int       `json:"id"`
    Name            string    `json:"name"`            // e.g., "GreenCastle"
    Program         string    `json:"program"`         // e.g., "claude-code"
    Model           string    `json:"model"`           // e.g., "opus-4.1"
    TaskDescription string    `json:"task_description"`
    LastActiveTS    time.Time `json:"last_active_ts"`
}

type Message struct {
    ID          int       `json:"id"`
    ThreadID    string    `json:"thread_id"`
    Subject     string    `json:"subject"`
    BodyMD      string    `json:"body_md"`
    From        string    `json:"from"`
    To          []string  `json:"to"`
    Importance  string    `json:"importance"`  // normal, high, urgent
    AckRequired bool      `json:"ack_required"`
    CreatedTS   time.Time `json:"created_ts"`
}

type FileReservation struct {
    ID          int       `json:"id"`
    PathPattern string    `json:"path_pattern"`
    AgentName   string    `json:"agent_name"`
    Exclusive   bool      `json:"exclusive"`
    Reason      string    `json:"reason"`
    ExpiresTS   time.Time `json:"expires_ts"`
}
```

### JSON-RPC 2.0 Protocol
Agent Mail uses JSON-RPC 2.0 over HTTP:
```go
type JSONRPCRequest struct {
    JSONRPC string      `json:"jsonrpc"` // "2.0"
    ID      interface{} `json:"id"`
    Method  string      `json:"method"`  // "tools/call"
    Params  struct {
        Name      string                 `json:"name"`
        Arguments map[string]interface{} `json:"arguments"`
    } `json:"params"`
}

type JSONRPCResponse struct {
    JSONRPC string          `json:"jsonrpc"`
    ID      interface{}     `json:"id"`
    Result  json.RawMessage `json:"result,omitempty"`
    Error   *JSONRPCError   `json:"error,omitempty"`
}
```

## Implementation Notes

- **Connection pooling**: Reuse HTTP connections; Agent Mail may be called frequently
- **Timeout handling**: 5s default for most operations, 30s for search/summarize
- **Retry logic**: Exponential backoff for transient failures (503, timeouts)
- **Bearer token**: Read from NTM config or AGENT_MAIL_TOKEN env var
- **Graceful degradation**: All methods return (result, error); callers handle missing server

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Client can connect to Agent Mail and verify health
- [ ] All MCP tools mapped to Go methods with proper types
- [ ] Error handling covers all Agent Mail error codes
- [ ] Unit tests with mock HTTP server
- [ ] Connection pooling verified with concurrent requests
- [ ] Timeout and retry behavior documented and tested","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:17:29.953845-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:26:20.670088-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:26:20.670088-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-cf8","title":"Add --json support to ntm deps command","description":"Implement JSON output for the deps (dependency check) command.

## What This Task Does

Add JSON support to deps command for scripted dependency checking.

## JSON Schema

{
  "dependencies": [
    {
      "name": "tmux",
      "required": true,
      "found": true,
      "version": "3.4",
      "path": "/opt/homebrew/bin/tmux"
    },
    {
      "name": "claude",
      "required": false,
      "found": true,
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "path": "/usr/local/bin/claude"
    }
  ],
  "all_required_met": true,
  "warnings": []
}

## Implementation

1. Create DepsOutput and DepInfo structs
2. Structure existing dependency checks
3. Include version detection where possible

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ntm deps --json produces valid JSON
- [ ] All dependencies listed with status
- [ ] Versions included where detectable
- [ ] Exit code reflects actual status

Shows sync status per agent.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverMountain","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:12.237009-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T09:15:45.186887-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T09:15:45.186887-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-f5ur","depends_on_id":"ntm-e12d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:37.332318-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-fpb","title":"Implement command history tracking","description":"Track and expose history of commands sent to agents.

## Rationale
For debugging, auditing, and understanding agent behavior, I need to know:
- What was sent to each agent and when?
- What was the response?
- How long did each command take?

This creates an audit trail and enables analysis.

## Storage
```go
type CommandEntry struct {
    ID        uint64
    Session   string
    Pane      string
    Command   string
    SentAt    time.Time
    AckAt     *time.Time       // when agent acked
    OutputLen int              // lines of output produced
    Duration  *time.Duration   // if completed
}
```

## Query Interface
```bash
ntm --robot-history myproject                    # all history
ntm --robot-history myproject --pane=0.1         # specific pane
ntm --robot-history myproject --last=10          # last N entries
ntm --robot-history myproject --since="1h ago"   # time-based
```

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "entries": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "pane": "0.1",
      "command": "implement feature X",
      "sent_at": "ISO8601",
      "ack_at": "ISO8601",
      "output_lines": 150,
      "duration_sec": 45
    }
  ],
  "total": 25
}
```

## Persistence
Store in SQLite DB per session:
~/.local/share/ntm/history/{session}.db

## Privacy Consideration
Commands may contain sensitive info. Consider:
- Optional encryption
- Configurable retention period
- Explicit --no-history flag","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:58:36.580871-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:58:36.580871-05:00"}

-{"id":"ntm-gcv","title":"Implement notification system with desktop and webhook support","description":"Create a notification system that can alert users via multiple channels.

## What This Task Does

Build infrastructure to send notifications when events occur:
- Desktop notifications (native OS)
- Webhook POST (Slack, Discord, custom)
- Shell command execution
- Log file appending

## Notification Events

- agent.error - Agent hit error state
- agent.crashed - Agent process exited
- agent.restarted - Agent was auto-restarted
- agent.idle - Agent waiting for input
- session.created - New session spawned
- session.killed - Session terminated
- health.degraded - Overall health dropped

## Configuration

[notifications]
enabled = true
events = ["agent.error", "agent.crashed", "agent.restarted"]

[notifications.desktop]
enabled = true

[notifications.webhook]
url = "https://hooks.slack.com/services/XXX/YYY/ZZZ"
# Payload template (Go template syntax)
template = '''
{"text": "NTM: {{.Event}} - {{.Message}}"}
'''

[notifications.shell]
command = "~/.config/ntm/notify.sh"
pass_json = true

[notifications.log]
path = "~/.local/share/ntm/notifications.jsonl"

## Implementation

Create internal/notify package:

type Event struct {
    Type      string
    Session   string
    Agent     string
    Message   string
    Timestamp time.Time
    Data      map[string]interface{}
}

type Notifier interface {
    Notify(event Event) error
}

Implementations:
- DesktopNotifier: osascript (macOS), notify-send (Linux)
- WebhookNotifier: HTTP POST with template rendering
- ShellNotifier: exec.Command with JSON stdin
- LogNotifier: Append to JSONL file

## Desktop Notifications

macOS:
osascript -e 'display notification "message" with title "NTM"'

Linux (notify-send):
notify-send "NTM" "message"

Windows/WSL:
powershell.exe -Command "[Windows.UI.Notifications...]"

## ntm notify command

Manual notification for testing/scripting:

$ ntm notify "Build complete" --session myproject
$ ntm notify --event agent.error --message "Rate limit hit"

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Desktop notifications work on macOS and Linux
- [ ] Webhook POST sends correctly formatted payload
- [ ] Shell command receives event as JSON
- [ ] Events can be filtered in config
- [ ] ntm notify command for manual triggers
- [ ] Errors in notification don't break NTM

- [ ] Shell command receives event as JSON
- [ ] Events can be filtered in config
- [ ] ntm notify command for manual triggers
- [ ] Errors in notification don't break NTM

 {"id":"ntm-gk5","title":"Integrate agent-mail into robot snapshot","description":"Include agent-mail status in unified snapshot.

## Rationale
Agent-mail is how agents coordinate. The snapshot needs to include mail status so orchestrator knows about pending communications.

## Integration Points
1. Check if agent-mail project exists for current working dir
2. Query agent-mail for registered agents
3. Map tmux panes to agent-mail identities
4. Include unread counts and pending ack counts

## Snapshot Extension
```json
{
  "...": "existing fields",
  "agent_mail": {
    "project": "/path/to/project",
    "agents": {
      "BlueLake": {"pane": "0.1", "unread": 2, "pending_ack": 1},
      "GreenCastle": {"pane": "0.2", "unread": 0, "pending_ack": 0}
    },
    "total_unread": 2,
    "threads_active": 3
  }
}
```

## Challenges
- Agent names in mail != pane identifiers (need mapping)
- Agent-mail may not be installed/configured
- Multiple projects may be active

## Graceful Degradation
If agent-mail not available, include:
```json
{"agent_mail": {"available": false, "reason": "not configured"}}
```","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FuchsiaHill","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:53:09.926646-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T04:27:49.182774-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-pp5.1.2","title":"Integrate history logging into ntm send command","description":"Modify the send command to log all prompts to history.

## Current State
internal/cli/send.go sends prompts but doesn't log them.

## Changes Required

### Import history package
```go
import "github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/history"
```

### Log after sending
```go
func runSend(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    // ... existing setup ...
    
    // Prepare history entry
    entry := history.HistoryEntry{
        ID:        history.NewID(),
        Timestamp: time.Now(),
        Session:   sessionName,
        Prompt:    prompt,
        Source:    "cli",
    }
    
    // Collect targets as we send
    var targets []string
    var sendErr error
    
    for _, pane := range selectedPanes {
        targets = append(targets, pane.Title)
        if err := sendToPane(pane, prompt); err \!= nil {
            sendErr = err
            // Don't break - continue to other panes
        }
    }
    
    // Complete entry
    entry.Targets = targets
    entry.Success = sendErr == nil
    if sendErr \!= nil {
        entry.Error = sendErr.Error()
    }
    
    // Log to history (ignore errors - don't fail send for history)
    if err := history.Append(entry); err \!= nil {
        // Log warning but don't fail
        fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to log to history: %v\n", err)
    }
    
    return sendErr
}
```

### Add --no-history flag
```go
cmd.Flags().Bool("no-history", false, "Don't log this prompt to history")
```

### Template Integration
If using --template, record template name:
```go
if templateName \!= "" {
    entry.Template = templateName
}
```

## Also Update Palette Send
internal/palette/model.go or wherever palette sends prompts:
- Same pattern: create entry, send, log
- Source = "palette"

## Testing
- Verify history.jsonl is created on first send
- Verify entry format matches schema
- Verify --no-history suppresses logging
- Verify errors are recorded but send continues

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every ntm send logs to history
- [ ] Palette sends also log to history  
- [ ] --no-history flag works
- [ ] Failed sends are logged with error
- [ ] Template name recorded when used","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:47:34.225928-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T04:27:36.819182-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T04:27:36.819182-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-pp5.1.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-pp5.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:47:34.227579-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-pp5.1.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-pp5.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:47:34.228462-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-pp5.2","title":"Prompt Replay Command","description":"New `ntm replay` command to re-send historical prompts.

## Command Interface
```bash
ntm replay 5                     # Re-send prompt #5 (from history)
ntm replay 01HXYZ...             # Re-send by ID
ntm replay --last                # Re-send most recent prompt
ntm replay 5 --to=cod            # Send to different targets
ntm replay 5 --session=other     # Send to different session
ntm replay 5 --edit              # Edit before sending
ntm replay 5 --dry-run           # Show what would be sent
```

## Lookup Behavior
1. If arg is a number, treat as index (1 = most recent)
2. If arg is a string, treat as ID prefix (match start)
3. `--last` is sugar for index 1

## Example Flow
```
$ ntm history
 #  TIME        SESSION      TARGETS         PROMPT (truncated)
 1  10:31:00    myproject    cc_1,cc_2       implement JWT authentication...
 2  10:30:00    myproject    cod_1           run all tests and fix failures
 3  10:25:00    otherproject gmi_1           analyze this codebase for...

$ ntm replay 1
Re-sending to myproject (cc_1, cc_2):
  "implement JWT authentication..."
Sent to 2 agents.

$ ntm replay 2 --to=cc --session=myproject
Re-sending to myproject (cc_1, cc_2):
  "run all tests and fix failures"
Sent to 2 agents.
```

## Edit Mode
When --edit is specified:
1. Write prompt to temp file
2. Open in 
3. Read modified content
4. Confirm and send

```go
func editPrompt(original string) (string, error) {
    f, _ := os.CreateTemp("", "ntm-prompt-*.md")
    f.WriteString(original)
    f.Close()
    
    editor := os.Getenv("EDITOR")
    if editor == "" { editor = "vim" }
    
    cmd := exec.Command(editor, f.Name())
    cmd.Stdin = os.Stdin
    cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
    cmd.Run()
    
    modified, _ := os.ReadFile(f.Name())
    return string(modified), nil
}
```

## Flags
| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| --last | Replay most recent prompt |
| --to | Override targets (--cc, --cod, --gmi, or pane names) |
| --session | Send to different session |
| --edit | Edit prompt before sending |
| --dry-run | Show what would be sent |
| --no-history | Don't log this replay |

## Files to Create
- internal/cli/replay.go - Command implementation

 {"id":"ntm-pp5.3","title":"Prompt Templates with Variables","description":"Named templates with variable substitution for reusable prompts.

## Overview
Templates are named, parameterized prompts stored in files.
Users can invoke them via `ntm send --template=name`.

## Storage Locations
1. Global: ~/.config/ntm/templates/*.md
2. Project: .ntm/templates/*.md (if Per-Project Config enabled)

## Template Format
```markdown
---
name: code_review
description: Review code for quality issues
variables:
  file: File path to review
  focus: What to focus on (optional)
---

Review the file {{file}} for:
- Code quality issues
- Potential bugs
- Performance concerns
{{#focus}}
Focus especially on: {{focus}}
{{/focus}}

Be thorough but concise.
```

## Variable Types

### Required Variables
`{{variable}}` - Must be provided via --var

### Optional Variables (with conditionals)
`{{#variable}}...{{/variable}}` - Section only included if variable provided

### Built-in Variables
| Variable | Value |
|----------|-------|
| `{{file}}` | Content of --file argument |
| `{{files}}` | Content of all --file arguments |
| `{{selection}}` | Current editor selection (if available) |
| `{{clipboard}}` | Clipboard content |
| `{{cwd}}` | Current working directory |
| `{{session}}` | Session name |
| `{{date}}` | Current date |
| `{{time}}` | Current time |

## Command Interface
```bash
# Use template
ntm send myproject --template=code_review --var file=src/auth.py

# Use with file injection
ntm send myproject -t code_review --file=src/auth.py
# {{file}} auto-populated with file contents

# List available templates
ntm template list

# Show template content
ntm template show code_review

# Create new template
ntm template create my_template
# Opens editor with scaffold
```

## Template Resolution
1. Look in project templates (.ntm/templates/)
2. Look in global templates (~/.config/ntm/templates/)
3. Look in built-in templates (embedded in binary)

## Built-in Templates
Ship with sensible defaults:
- code_review: Review code for issues
- explain: Explain how code works
- refactor: Refactor code
- test: Write tests
- document: Add documentation

## Parsing Implementation
```go
func ParseTemplate(content string, vars map[string]string) (string, error) {
    // Parse frontmatter
    meta, body := splitFrontmatter(content)
    
    // Check required variables
    for _, v := range meta.Variables {
        if _, ok := vars[v.Name]; \!ok && v.Required {
            return "", fmt.Errorf("missing required variable: %s", v.Name)
        }
    }
    
    // Expand conditionals
    body = expandConditionals(body, vars)
    
    // Substitute variables
    body = substituteVariables(body, vars)
    
    return body, nil
}
```

## Files to Create
- internal/templates/loader.go - Find and load templates
- internal/templates/parser.go - Parse template syntax
- internal/templates/builtin.go - Embedded default templates
- internal/cli/template.go - Template management commands","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:04.28723-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:04.28723-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-pp5.3","depends_on_id":"ntm-pp5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:04.287592-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"ntm-pp5.4","title":"History Command (ntm history)","description":"CLI command to view and search prompt history.

## Command Interface
```bash
ntm history                          # Show recent prompts
ntm history --session=myproject      # Filter by session
ntm history --limit=50               # Show last 50
ntm history --since=1h               # Last hour
ntm history --search='auth'          # Search prompt text
ntm history --json                   # Output as JSON
ntm history clear                    # Clear all history
ntm history clear --before=7d        # Clear older than 7 days
```

## Default Output
```
 #  TIME        SESSION      TARGETS     PROMPT
 1  10:31:00    myproject    cc_1,cc_2   implement JWT authentication for the API...
 2  10:30:00    myproject    cod_1       run all tests and fix any failures that...
 3  10:25:00    backend      gmi_1       analyze this codebase for security vulne...
 4  10:20:00    myproject    all (5)     review the changes and suggest improveme...
 5  10:15:00    frontend     cc_1        create a React component for user login...

Showing 5 of 127 entries. Use --limit for more.
```

## Detailed View
```bash
ntm history show 1
```
```
ID:       01HXYZ...
Time:     2025-12-10 10:31:00
Session:  myproject
Targets:  cc_1, cc_2
Source:   cli
Template: (none)

Prompt:
  implement JWT authentication for the API. Include:
  - Token generation
  - Token validation
  - Refresh token flow
  - Middleware for protected routes

Status: Success
```

## Flags
| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| --limit, -n | Number of entries (default: 20) |
| --session, -s | Filter by session name |
| --since | Time filter (1h, 1d, 1w) |
| --search | Search prompt text |
| --source | Filter by source (cli, palette, replay) |
| --json | JSON output |

## Subcommands
- `ntm history` - List (default)
- `ntm history show <id>` - Show details
- `ntm history clear` - Clear history
- `ntm history export` - Export to file

## Implementation
```go
func newHistoryCmd() *cobra.Command {
    cmd := &cobra.Command{
        Use:   "history",
        Short: "View prompt history",
        RunE:  runHistoryList,
    }
    
    cmd.AddCommand(
        &cobra.Command{Use: "show", RunE: runHistoryShow},
        &cobra.Command{Use: "clear", RunE: runHistoryClear},
        &cobra.Command{Use: "export", RunE: runHistoryExport},
    )
    
    return cmd
}
```

 {"id":"ntm-qxp","title":"Implement command plugins as executable scripts","description":"Allow users to add custom commands via executable scripts.

## What This Task Does

Enable users to create custom NTM commands by placing executable
scripts in ~/.config/ntm/commands/. Any executable becomes a subcommand.

## Plugin Location

~/.config/ntm/commands/
├── sync-all*         # ntm sync-all
├── morning-setup*    # ntm morning-setup  
└── deploy*           # ntm deploy

## Script Interface

Scripts receive NTM context via environment variables:
- NTM_SESSION: Current/specified session name
- NTM_JSON: Set to '1' if --json flag passed
- NTM_CONFIG_PATH: Path to NTM config file
- NTM_VERSION: NTM version string

Scripts can use ntm commands internally:
#\!/bin/bash
# ~/.config/ntm/commands/sync-all
for session in $(ntm list --json | jq -r '.[].name'); do
  ntm send "$session" --all "git pull && npm install"
done

## Command Discovery

On startup:
1. Scan ~/.config/ntm/commands/
2. Check each file is executable
3. Register as cobra subcommand
4. Extract description from script header comment

Script description convention:
#\!/bin/bash
# Description: Sync all agents with latest code
# Usage: ntm sync-all [--force]

## Integration with Help

$ ntm --help
...
Plugin Commands:
  sync-all      Sync all agents with latest code
  morning-setup Set up morning development session
  deploy        Deploy current branch to staging

$ ntm sync-all --help
# Runs: script --help

## Arguments and Flags

Pass through all arguments to script:
$ ntm deploy --env staging --force
# Runs: ~/.config/ntm/commands/deploy --env staging --force

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Executables in commands/ become subcommands
- [ ] Environment variables passed to scripts
- [ ] Arguments passed through
- [ ] Description parsed from header
- [ ] Help shows plugin commands
- [ ] Non-executable files ignored with warning
